verses.text: For the chief musician; of David The fool says in his heart, “No God.” They are corrupt, they do a deed abominably. There is none doing good. 2 Yahweh looks down from the heavens on the sons of man to see whether there is one understanding, one seeking God. 3 The whole has turned aside. Together they have corrupted themselves. There is none doing good, not even one. 4 Do they not know, all of the doers of iniquity, the ones eating my people as they eat bread? They do not call upon Yahweh.
There they feared a fear, for God is with the generation of the righteous. 6 You shame the counsel of the afflicted, but Yahweh is his refuge.
Who will give the salvation of Israel from Zion? When Yahweh restores the prosperity of his people, Jacob will be glad, Israel will rejoice.

Of David Do not be upset about evildoers; do not be envious of the doers of unrighteousness. 2 For like the grass they will quickly wither, and like a green plant they will fade. 3 Trust in Yahweh and do good; inhabit the land and feed faithfulness. 4 And delight yourself in Yahweh, and he will give to you the desires of your heart. 5 Roll your way upon Yahweh, and trust in him, and he, he will act 6 and he will bring out your righteousness like the light, and your justice like the noonday. 7 Be silent to Yahweh and tremble for him. Do not be angry because of the one making his way prosper, because of a man doing schemes. 8 Let go of nose and abandon wrath; do not be angry; only to cause evil. 9 For evildoers will be cut off, but the ones waiting for Yahweh, they, they will inherit the land. 10 And yet a little and there are no wicked; then you will look carefully at his place, but he is not. 11 But the meek will inherit the land and will delight themselves over an abundance of peace. 12 The wicked is plotting against the righteous and is gnashing his teeth against him. 13 The Lord laughs at him, for he sees that his day is coming. 14 The wicked have opened a sword and have trodden their bow to bring down the poor and the needy, to slaughter the upright of way. 15 Their sword will come into their heart, and their bows will be broken. 16 Better is the little of the righteous than the abundance of many wicked. 17 For the arms of the wicked will be broken, but Yahweh is supporting the righteous. 18 Yahweh is knowing the days of the blameless, and their inheritance will be forever. 19 They will not have shame in a time of evil, and in the days of famine they will have enough. 20 For the wicked will perish, and the enemies of Yahweh will be like the precious of pastures; they vanish, in smoke they vanish. 21 The wicked is borrowing and does not repay, but the righteous is showing grace and giving. 22 For those blessed by him will inherit the land, but those cursed by him will be cut off. 23 From Yahweh the steps of a man are established, and he delights in his way. 24 Though he falls, he will not be hurled down, for Yahweh is supporting his hand. 25 I was a youth, and I am old, and I have not seen the righteous abandoned or his seed seeking bread. 26 All the day he is showing grace and lending, and his seed are for a blessing. 27 Turn away from evil and do good, and dwell forever. 28 For Yahweh is loving justice, and he will not abandon his faithful. Forever they are preserved, but the offspring of the wicked will be cut off. 29 The righteous will inherit the land and will dwell forever upon it. 30 The mouth of the righteous murmurs wisdom, and his tongue speaks justice. 31 The law of his God is in his heart; his steps will not slip. 32 The wicked is watching the righteous and is seeking to kill him. 33 Yahweh will not abandon him into his hand, and will not condemn him when he is judged. 34 Wait for Yahweh and keep his way, and he will raise you up to inherit the land. When the wicked are cut off, you will see it. 35 I have seen a wicked, violent one, and spreading himself out like a green native tree. 36 And he passed away, and behold, he was not, and I sought him, and he was not found. 37 Watch the blameless and see the upright, for there is a future for the man of peace. 38 But the transgressors are destroyed together; the future of the wicked is cut off. 39 And the salvation of the righteous is from Yahweh, their fortress in the time of distress. 40 And Yahweh helps them and delivers them; he delivers them from the wicked and saves them, because they have taken refuge in him.

For the chief musician; a song; a psalm. Shout to God, all of the earth! 2 Make music to the glory of his name; make his praise glorious. 3 Say to God, “How fearsome are your deeds! Because of the greatness of your power, your enemies cringe before you. 4 All of the earth bows down to you and makes music to you; they make music to your name.” Selah 5 Come and see the works of God, fearsome in doing toward the sons of man. 6 He turned the sea into dry land; they passed through the river on foot. There let us rejoice in him! 7 He is ruling by his might forever; his eyes watch over the nations. Let the ones rebelling not exalt themselves. Selah 8 Bless our God, peoples, and make the sound of his praise heard, 9 the one putting our soul among the living, and he does not allow our foot to slip. 10 For you tested us, God; you refined us like the refining of silver. 11 You brought us into a net; you put pressure on our loins. 12 You made man ride over our head; we went through fire and through waters, but you brought us out to abundance. 13 I will come into your house with burnt offerings; I will pay my vows to you, 14 for which my lips opened and my mouth spoke when trouble was to me. 15 I will offer up burnt offerings of fatlings to you with the smoke of rams; I will offer cattle with male goats. Selah 16 Come, listen, and let me recount, all fearers of God, what he has done for my soul. 17 I called to him with my mouth, and he was exalted from my tongue. 18 If I had regarded iniquity in my heart, the Lord would not have heard. 19 Surely God has heard; he has attended to the voice of my prayer. 20 Blessed be God, who has not turned away my prayer or his covenant faithfulness from me.

In you, Yahweh, I take refuge; let me not ever have shame. 2 In your righteousness, deliver me and rescue me; incline your ear to me and save me. 3 Be to me a rock of abode for coming continually; you command to save me, for you are my rock and my stronghold. 4 My God, rescue me from the hand of the wicked, from the palm of the one doing wrong and the one being cruel; 5 for you are my hope, Lord Yahweh, my confidence from my youth. 6 I have supported myself on you from the womb; you were taking me from the belly of my mother. My praise is about you continually. 7 I have become like a wonder to many, but you are my refuge of strength. 8 Let my mouth be filled with your praise, all of the day with your splendor. 9 May you not cast me away in the time of old age; may you not forsake me when my strength fails. 10 For my enemies have spoken against me, and the ones watching my life have consulted together, 11 saying, “God has forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is none delivering.” 12 God, may you not be far from me; my God, hasten to my help. 13 Let them shame and finish, the adversaries of my life; let them cover with reproach and disgrace, the ones seeking my harm. 14 But I, I will hope continually, and I will add to all of your praise. 15 My mouth will recount your righteousness, all of the day, your salvation, for I do not know the numbers. 16 I will come in the forces of Lord Yahweh; I will remember your righteousness, yours alone. 17 God, you have taught me from my youth, and to this I declare your wonderful doings. 18 And also until old age and gray hair, God, do not forsake me, until I declare your arm to a generation, your might to everyone who will come. 19 For your righteousness, God, is to height, you who do great things; God, who is like you? 20 You who have made me see many and evil troubles, may you return, may you revive me, and may you return, may you bring me up from the depths of the earth. 21 May you increase my greatness, and may you turn around, may you comfort me. 22 Also I, I will praise you with the instrument of the lute for your faithfulness, my God; Let me make music to you with the harp, Holy One of Israel. 23 My lips will shout joyfully when I make music to you, and my soul, which you have redeemed. 24 Also my tongue will meditate on your righteousness all of the day, for they will shame, for they will confound the ones seeking my harm.

A maskil. Of Asaph. Why, God, have you rejected us forever? Why does your nose smoke against the flock of your pasture? 2 Remember your congregation that you acquired in antiquity, which you redeemed, the tribe of your inheritance, this Mount Zion on which you have dwelt. 3 Lift up your feet to the destructions of perpetuity, to all the enemy has damaged in the holy place. 4 Your adversaries roared in the midst of your meeting place; they set up their signs as signs. 5 It was known like one bringing upward axes in a thicket of trees. 6 And now with hatchet and hammers they have broken down its carvings altogether. 7 They sent your holy place into fire; they profaned the dwelling of your name to the ground. 8 They said in their heart, “Let us oppress them altogether.” They burned all of the meeting places of God in the land. 9 We do not see our signs; there is no longer a prophet and not one with us knowing until when. 10 Until when, God, will the adversary reproach? Will the enemy despise your name forever? 11 Why do you hold back your hand, even your right hand? From the midst of your bosom finish them! 12 But God is my king from antiquity, working salvations in the midst of the earth. 13 You, you divided the sea by your strength; you broke the heads of the sea monsters in the waters. 14 You, you crushed the heads of Leviathan; you gave him as food to a people, to the desert-dwellers. 15 You, you split open spring and wadi; you, you dry up perpetual rivers. 16 The day is to you; the night is also to you. You, you established the light and the sun. 17 You, you set all of the boundaries of the earth; summer and winter, you, you formed them. 18 Remember this: the enemy has reproached Yahweh, and a foolish people has despised your name. 19 May you not give the life of your dove to a beast; may you not forget the life of your poor forever. 20 Regard the covenant, for pastures of violence fill the dark places of the land. 21 May the weak not return ashamed; may the poor and the needy praise your name. 22 Arise, God! Plead your case! Remember your reproach from the fool all of the day. 23 Do not forget the voice of your adversaries, the uproar of the ones rising against you, going up continually.

A maskil of Asaph. Hear my teaching, my people; incline your ears to the words of my mouth. 2 I will open my mouth in a parable; I will pour out riddles from antiquity. 3 These are things that we have heard and learned, things that our fathers have told us. 4 We will not keep them from their descendants. We will tell the next generation about the praises of Yahweh, his strength, and his wonders that he has done. 5 And he established a testimony in Jacob and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers to teach them to their children, 6 So that the later generation might know, the children yet to be born, and they will rise up and tell to their children. 7 And they would place their confidence in God and not forget the deeds of God but keep his commandments. 8 And they would not be like their fathers, a stubborn generation and a rebellious generation, which did not cause its heart to be steadfast, and its spirit was not faithful with God. 9 The sons of Ephraim being armed with shooters of bows, they turned back on the day of battle. 10 They did not keep the covenant of God, and they refused to walk in his law. 11 And they forgot his deeds, and his wonders that he had shown them. 12 He did wonders before their fathers in the land of Egypt, in the territory of Zoan. 13 He divided the sea and led them across it, and he made the waters to stand like a heap. 14 And by day he led them with the cloud and all the night with the light of fire. 15 He split open the rocks in the wilderness, and he gave drink abundantly as the deep. 16 He made streams flow out of the rock and made the water flow like rivers. 17 And they added still to sin against him, to rebel against the Most High in the dry land. 18 And they tested God in their hearts by asking for food for their appetite. 19 And they spoke against God. They said, “Can God arrange a table in the wilderness? 20 Behold, he struck the rock and waters gushed out and streams overflowed. Is he able to give bread also? Or can he provide meat for his people?” 21 Therefore, Yahweh heard and was furious, and a fire was kindled against Jacob, and also his nose went up against Israel, 22 because they did not believe in God and did not trust in his salvation. 23 Yet he commanded the clouds from above and opened the doors of the sky. 24 And he rained down manna to eat on them, and gave to them the grain of heaven. 25 Man ate the bread of the mighty. He sent to them provision to their satisfaction. 26 He caused the east wind to blow in the sky, and by his power he guided the south wind. 27 And he rained down meat on them like dust, and birds of wing as the sands of the sea. 28 And he caused them to fall in the middle of his camp, around his dwellings. 29 And they ate and were very satisfied. And he brought their craving to them. 30 They were not separated from their craving, their food was still in their mouth, 31 and the nose of God went up in them and he killed some of their stoutest people, and he caused the young men of Israel to bow down. 32 In all this they sinned again, and did not believe in his wonders. 33 So he consumed their days in a vapor, and their years in terror. 34 When he killed them, then they would seek him, and they would return and look earnestly for God. 35 And they would remember that God was their rock and that God Most High was their redeemer. 36 And they would deceive him with their mouth and lie to him with their tongues. 37 And their hearts were not steadfast with him, and they were not faithful in his covenant. 38 Yet he, being merciful, atoned for their iniquity and did not destroy them. And many times he held back his nose and did not stir up all his wrath. 39 And he remembered that they were flesh, a wind going and it does not return. 40 How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness! They grieved him in the desert! 41 And they returned and they tested God and grieved the Holy One of Israel. 42 They did not remember his hand, the day when he redeemed them from the adversary, 43 when he set his signs in Egypt and his wonders in the field of Zoan. 44 And he turned their rivers to blood and their streams they could not drink. 45 He sent a swarm among them, and it devoured them, and the frog and it destroyed them. 46 And he gave their produce to the locust and their labor to the swarming locust. 47 He slew their vines with hail and their sycamore trees with frost. 48 And he gave over their cattle to the hail and their livestock to the bolts of lightning. 49 He sent among them the burning of his nose, fury and indignation and distress, a group of messengers of calamity. 50 He made level a path for his anger; he did not spare their soul from death, and their life he gave over to the plague. 51 And he struck every firstborn in Egypt, the beginning of strength in the tents of Ham. 52 And he made his people journey like sheep, and he led them like a flock in the wilderness. 53 And he guided them in security, and they did not fear, and the sea covered their enemies. 54 And he brought them to the border of his holiness, this mountain that his right hand acquired. 55 And he drove out nations from before them and caused them to fall in a portion of inheritance, and he caused the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents. 56 But they tested and rebelled against God Most High, and they did not keep his testimonies. 57 And they turned back and acted treacherously like their fathers; they turned themselves like a bow of deceit. 58 And they provoked him to anger with their high places, and with their carved images they moved him to jealousy. 59 God heard and was furious, and he rejected Israel greatly. 60 And he abandoned the dwelling of Shiloh, the tent in which he had dwelt among mankind. 61 And he gave his strength to captivity and his splendor into the hand of the enemy. 62 And he delivered his people over to the sword, and he was furious with his inheritance. 63 Fire consumed his young men, and his virgins were not praised in wedding songs. 64 His priests fell by the sword, and his widows did not weep. 65 Then the Lord awoke as one sleeping, as a mighty man shouting from wine. 66 And he struck his adversaries backward; he gave to them a reproach of perpetuity. 67 And he rejected the tent of Joseph, and he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim. 68 But he chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion, which he loved. 69 And he built his sanctuary like the heights, like the earth he founded it forever. 70 And he chose David his servant and took him from the folds of the flock. 71 From behind the nursing ewes he brought him, to shepherd Jacob his people and Israel his inheritance. 72 And he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart, and with the skillfulness of his hands he guided them.

A psalm. Of Asaph. God, nations have come into your inheritance; they have defiled the temple of your holiness; they have made Jerusalem into heaps. 2 They have given the corpse of your servants as food to the bird of the heavens, the flesh of your faithful to the beast of the earth. 3 They have poured out their blood like water around Jerusalem, and there is no burying. 4 We have become a reproach to our neighbors, scorn and derision to the ones around us. 5 Until when, Yahweh? Will you be angry forever? Will your jealousy burn like fire? 6 Pour out your wrath on the nations that have not known you and on the kingdoms that have not called on your name. 7 For one has devoured Jacob, and they have desolated his habitation. 8 Do not remember former iniquities against us; hasten, let your compassions meet us, for we have become very weak. 9 Help us, God of our salvation, on account of the glory of your name, and deliver us and cover over our sins for the sake of your name. 10 Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?” Let the vengeance of the poured-out blood of your servants be known among the nations to our eyes. 11 Let the groaning of the prisoner come to your face; according to the greatness of your arm, preserve the sons of death. 12 And return to our neighbors sevenfold, into their bosom, their reproach with which they have reproached you, Lord. 13 And we, your people and the flock of your pasture, will give thanks to you forever; to generation and generation we will recount your praise.

A psalm. Of Asaph. God is standing in the congregation of God; in the midst of gods he judges: 2 “Until when will you judge with injustice and lift up the faces of the wicked? Selah 3 Judge the poor and the orphan; vindicate the poor and the destitute. 4 Rescue the poor and the needy; deliver them from the hand of the wicked. 5 They do not know and they do not understand; they walk about in darkness. All of the foundations of the earth are shaken. 6 I, I said, ‘You are gods and sons of the Most High, all of you. 7 But you will die like man, and like one of the princes, you will fall.’” 8 Arise, God, judge the earth, for you, you will inherit among all of the nations.

A prayer. Of David. Incline your ear, Yahweh; answer me, for I am poor and needy. 2 Guard my soul, for I am faithful. You, my God, save your servant, the one trusting in you. 3 Favor me, Lord, for to you I call all of the day. 4 Gladden the soul of your servant, for to you, Lord, I lift up my soul. 5 For you, Lord, are good and forgiving and plentiful of covenant faithfulness to all of the ones calling on you. 6 Listen to my prayer, Yahweh, and attend to the voice of my pleas. 7 In the day of my trouble I call on you, for you will answer me. 8 There is none like you among the gods, Lord, and there is nothing like your works. 9 All of the nations that you have made will come and bow down to your face, Lord, and they will glorify your name. 10 For you are great and are doing wonderful things; you are God, you alone. 11 Teach me your way, Yahweh. that I may walk in your truth. Unite my heart to fear your name. 12 I will thank you, Lord my God, with all of my heart, and I will glorify your name forever. 13 For your covenant faithfulness is great toward me, and you have delivered my soul from lowest Sheol. 14 God, the arrogant arise against me, and the congregation of the violent seek my life, and they do not set you before them. 15 But you, Lord, are a merciful and gracious God, long of nostrils and plentiful of covenant faithfulness and truth. 16 Turn to me and favor me; give your strength to your servant, and save the son of your female servant. 17 Work for me a sign for good, that the ones hating me may see and have shame, for you, Yahweh, you help me and comfort me.

Of the sons of Korah. A psalm. A song. His foundation is on the mountains of holiness. 2 Yahweh is loving the gates of Zion more than all of the dwellings of Jacob. 3 Glorious things are spoken of you, city of God. Selah 4 I will mention Rahab and Babylon to the ones knowing me; behold, Philistia and Tyre with Cush— “This one was born there.” 5 And of Zion it will be said, “Man and man was born in her, and he, he will establish her, the Most High.” 6 Yahweh will recount in the writing of the peoples, “This one was born there.” Selah 7 And the singers, like the pipers, “All of my springs are in you.”

Rise up, judge of the earth, return recompense upon the proud. 3 Until when will the wicked, Yahweh, until when will the wicked rejoice? 4 They pour out, they speak, arrogance; all doers of evil praise themselves. 5 They crush your people, Yahweh; they afflict your inheritance. 6 They kill the widow and the sojourner, and they murder the fatherless. 7 They say, “Yah does not see, the God of Jacob does not perceive.” 8 Understand, stupid ones among the people! O fools, when will you have insight? 9 The one planting the ear, does he not hear? Or the one forming the eye, does he not observe? 10 The one instructing nations, does he not rebuke? Does the one teaching mankind lack knowledge? 11 Yahweh knows the thoughts of mankind, that they are vapor. 12 The joys of the man whom you instruct, Yah, and from your law you teach him, 13 to give rest to him from days of evil until a pit is dug for the wicked. 14 For Yahweh will not forsake his people and he will not abandon his inheritance. 15 For judgment will return to the right; and all the upright in heart are after it. 16 Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? Who will stand up for me against the workers of wickedness? 17 Unless Yahweh had been a help to me, shortly my soul would have dwelt in silence. 18 When I said, “My foot is slipping,” Your covenant faithfulness, Yahweh, supported me. 19 When my cares within me are many, your consolations gladden my soul. 20 Can a throne of destruction be your ally, one creating injustice by statute? 21 They conspire together against the life of the righteous and they condemn innocent blood. 22 But Yahweh has been to me a high tower, and my God, the rock of my refuge. 23 He will return on them their iniquity and he will destroy them in their wickedness. Yahweh our God will destroy them.

A Psalm of David I shall sing of covenant faithfulness and justice; to you, Yahweh, I shall sing praises. 2 I shall study the blameless way. When will you come to me? I will walk in the integrity of my heart within my house. 3 I will not put a worthless thing before my eyes; I hate to do transgressions; it will not cling to me. 4 A perverse heart will turn away from me; I will not know evil. 5 The one slandering his neighbor in secret, him I will destroy. The one lofty of eyes and the one wide of heart, him I cannot. 6 My eyes are on the faithful ones of the land to sit with me; The one walking in the way of integrity, he may serve me. 7 A doer of deceit will not remain within my house; a speaker of lies will not be established before my eyes. 8 In the mornings I will destroy all the wicked of the land, to cut off all workers of evil from the city of Yahweh.

To the chief musician: a psalm of David. God of my praise, do not be silent! 2 For a wicked mouth and a mouth of deceit open against me; they speak a tongue of untruth with me, 3 and words of hatred surround me, and they attack me without cause. 4 In return for my love they accuse me, but I—prayer. 5 They put upon me evil in return for good, and hatred in return for my love. 6 Appoint a wicked one over him; and may an accuser stand at his right hand. 7 When he is judged, may he come out guilty; and may his prayer be as sin. 8 May his days be few; may another take his position. 9 May his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow. 10 And wandering, may his children wander and beg and seek from their desolations. 11 May a creditor snare all that is to him; may strangers plunder his work. 12 May there not be to him one extending covenant faithfulness; and may there not be one pitying his orphans. 13 May his posterity be to cut off; may their name be blotted out in the next generation. 14 May the sin of his fathers be remembered to Yahweh; and the offense of his mother not be blotted out. 15 May they be before Yahweh continually and may he cut off their remembrance from the earth, 16 because he never remembered to do covenant faithfulness, but pursued the humble man and the needy and the dejected in heart to death. 17 And he loved cursing, and it came to him, and he did not delight in blessing, and it was far from him. 18 And he put on cursing as his garment, and it entered into his inner being like water, and like oil into his bones. 19 May it be to him like the clothing he wears, and for the belt he always girds himself with it. 20 This, the payment of my accusers from Yahweh, and those speaking evil against my soul. 21 But you, Yahweh, my Lord, do with me on account of your name. Because your covenant faithfulness is good, rescue me! 22 For I am poor and needy, and my heart breaks within me. 23 Like a shadow when it lengthens I am made to go; I am shaken off like a locust. 24 My knees are weak from fasting and my flesh is gaunt from fatness. 25 And I, I am a disgrace to them; they see me; they shake their head. 26 Help me, Yahweh my God; save me according to your covenant faithfulness, 27 so that they know that this is your hand; you, Yahweh, you have done it. 28 They, they will curse, but you, you will bless; they arise, but then become ashamed, and your servant will rejoice. 29 May my accusers be clothed with disgrace; may they wear their shame like a robe. 30 With my mouth I greatly thank Yahweh; and in the midst of many I will praise him. 31 For he stands at the right hand of the needy, to save him from the judges of his soul.

Of David, a psalm. The declaration of Yahweh to my lord the king: “Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.” 2 Yahweh will stretch out the scepter of your strength from Zion; rule in the midst of your enemies! 3 Your people, freewill offerings on the day of your strength, in the beauty of holiness; from the womb of the dawn, the dew of your youth is to you. 4 Yahweh has sworn, and will not revoke it: “You are a priest forever, after the manner of Melchizedek.” 5 The Lord is at your right hand. He crushes kings on the day of his nose. 6 He will judge the nations; he fills with bodies, he crushes the head, upon abundant land. 7 He will drink from the stream along the path, Therefore, he will lift up the head.
Rather, I have stilled and quieted my soul like a weaned child upon his mother; like a weaned child upon me is my soul. 3 Israel, hope in Yahweh from now and until forever!

A song of ascents; of David Behold, how good and how pleasant is the dwelling of brothers, yes, together! 2 Like the good oil on the head, coming down on the beard, the beard of Aaron, that is coming down on the mouth of his garments, 3 like the dew of Hermon that is coming down on the mountains of Zion, for there Yahweh commanded the blessing, life to forever.

A song of the ascents. Behold, bless Yahweh, all servants of Yahweh, the ones standing in the house of Yahweh in the nights. 2 Lift up your hands to the holiness, and bless Yahweh. 3 May Yahweh bless you from Zion, the maker of heaven and earth.

For the chief musician; of David; a psalm. Yahweh, you have searched me and you know me. 2 You, you know my sitting down and my rising up; you understand my thought from far away. 3 My path and my lying down you have winnowed, and you are familiar with all of my ways. 4 For there is not a word on my tongue; behold, Yahweh, you know all of it. 5 Behind and before you have enclosed me, and you have set your palm upon me. 6 Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high; I am not capable of it. 7 Where shall I go from your spirit? And where shall I flee from your face? 8 If I ascend to the heavens, you are there, and I spread out in Sheol, behold, you! 9 If I lift up the wings of the dawn, dwell in the farthest part of the sea, 10 even there your hand will lead me, and your right hand will hold me. 11 And I said, “Surely darkness will crush me, and night, the light around me.” 12 Even darkness does not make it dark to you, and the night shines like the day; like darkness, like light. 13 For you, you created my kidneys; you knit me together in the womb of my mother. 14 I will thank you because fearsomely, I am wonderfully made. Wonderful are your works, and my soul knows it very well. 15 My frame was not hidden from you when I was made in the secret place, when I was woven in the depths of the earth, 16 your eyes saw my unformed substance. And in your book all of them were written: the days were formed, and yet not one of them was. 17 And to me, how precious are your thoughts, God! How numerous are their heads! 18 If I should count them, they are more than the sand. I awake, and I am still with you. 19 If only you would kill the wicked, God! And men of bloodshed, depart from me, 20 who speak of you for an evil purpose; they lift up your name to emptiness—your enemies. 21 Do I not hate the ones hating you, Yahweh? And do I not loathe the ones rising up against you? 22 With a completeness of hatred I hate them; they have become enemies to me. 23 Search me, God, and know my heart; examine me and know my cares. 24 And see if a way of hurtfulness is in me, and lead me in the way of eternity.

Of David. Blessed be Yahweh, my rock, the one teaching my hands for war, my fingers for battle, 2 my covenant faithfulness and my fortress, my stronghold and the one rescuing me, my shield, and in whom I take refuge, the one subduing my people under me. 3 Yahweh, what is man, that you know him, the son of man, that you consider him? 4 Man compares to vapor; his days are like a passing shadow. 5 Yahweh, bow your heavens and come down; touch the mountains, that they may smoke. 6 Light lightning and scatter them; send your arrows and rout them. 7 Send your hands from above; break me free and deliver me from many waters, from the hand of sons of a foreigner, 8 who, their mouth speaks worthlessness, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood. 9 God, I will sing a new song to you; with a lyre of ten strings I will make music to you, 10 the one giving salvation to kings, the one breaking free David his servant from the evil sword. 11 Break me free and deliver me from the hand of the sons of a foreigner, who, their mouth speaks worthlessness, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood, 12 that our sons may be like plants having been enlarged in their youth, our daughters, like corner pillars carved in the pattern of a palace. 13 May our granaries be full, furnishing from kind to kind; may our flocks be thousanding, may they be myriading in our fields. 14 May our oxen be burdened; no breach and no going out and no cry in our squares. 15 The happinesses of the people for whom it is thus to it! The happinesses of the people for whom Yahweh is its God!

A psalm of David. I will exalt you, my God, the King, and I will bless your name forever and ever. 2 I will bless you every day, and I will praise your name forever and ever. 3 Yahweh is great and being much praised, and there is no searching of his greatness. 4 Generation to generation will proclaim your works, and they will declare your mighty acts. 5 On the splendor of the glory of your majesty and on the deeds of your making wonderful, let me meditate. 6 And they will tell the strength of your making fearful, and let me recount it, your greatness. 7 They will pour forth the remembrance of the abundance of your goodness, and they will sing joyfully about your righteousness. 8 Yahweh is gracious and compassionate, long of nostrils and great of covenant faithfulness. 9 Yahweh is good to all, and his compassions are over all of his works. 10 All your works will thank you, Yahweh, and your faithful will bless you. 11 They will tell the glory of your kingdom, and they will speak of your power, 12 to make known to the sons of man his mighty acts and the glory of the splendor of his kingdom. 13 Your kingdom is a kingdom of all of eternities, and your dominion is in every generation and generation. 14 Yahweh is supporting all of the ones falling and raising up all of the ones being bowed down. 15 The eyes of all wait for you, and you, you are giving their food to them in its time, 16 opening your hand and satisfying every living thing with respect to desire. 17 Yahweh is righteous in all of his ways and faithful in all of his works. 18 Yahweh is near to all of the ones calling on him, to all who call on him in truth. 19 He does the desire of the ones fearing him, and he hears their cry, and he saves them. 20 Yahweh is keeping all of the ones loving him, but he destroys all of the wicked. 21 My mouth will speak the praise of Yahweh, and all flesh will bless the name of his holiness forever and ever.

Hallelu Yah! Praise Yahweh, my soul! 2 Let me praise Yahweh in my life; let me make music to my God while I yet am. 3 Do not trust in nobles, in the son of man, in whom there is no salvation. 4 His breath goes out, he returns to his ground; on that day, his thoughts perish. 5 The happinesses of the one who has the God of Jacob for his help; his hope is in Yahweh his God, 6 the one having made the heavens and the earth, the sea and all that is in them, the one keeping faithfulness forever, 7 doing justice for the oppressed, giving bread to the hungry. Yahweh is releasing the bound ones. 8 Yahweh is opening the blind; Yahweh is raising up the one bowed down; Yahweh is loving the righteous. 9 Yahweh is guarding the sojourners; he upholds the orphan and the widow, but the way of the wicked, he overturns. 10 Yahweh reigns forever; your God, Zion, to generation and generation. Hallelu Yah!

Hallelu Yah! For it is good to make music to our God, for praise is pleasant and suitable. 2 Yahweh builds up Jerusalem; he gathers the scattered of Israel, 3 healing the broken of heart and binding up their wounds; 4 counting the number of the stars, he calls names to all of them. 5 Great is our Lord, and abundant of power, his understanding is without number. 6 Yahweh is lifting up the poor, bringing down the wicked to the ground. 7 Sing to Yahweh with thanksgiving; make music to our God with the harp, 8 the one covering the heavens with clouds; the one preparing rain for the earth, the one making the mountains sprout with grass. 9 He gives its food to the beast, to the sons of the raven when they call. 10 He does not delight in the strength of a horse; he does not take pleasure in the legs of a man. 11 Yahweh takes pleasure in the ones fearing him, the ones hoping for his covenant faithfulness. 12 Extol Yahweh, Jerusalem; praise your God, Zion. 13 For he strengthens the bars of your gates; he blesses your sons in your midst. 14 The one putting peace at your borders, he satisfies you with the fat of wheat. 15 The one sending his command to the earth, his word runs to swiftness. 16 The one making snow like wool, he scatters frost like ashes. 17 The one sending his hail like crumbs, who can stand to the face of his cold? 18 He sends his word, and he makes them melt; he makes his wind blow, and the waters flow. 19 He declared his word to Jacob, his statutes and his judgments to Israel. 20 He has not done this for any other nation, and his judgments, they do not know them. Hallelu Yah!

Hallelu Yah! Praise Yahweh from the heavens; praise him in the heights. 2 Praise him, all of his angels; praise him, all of his armies. 3 Praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all of the stars of light. 4 Praise him, heavens of the heavens, and the waters that are above the heavens. 5 Let them praise the name of Yahweh, for he, he commanded, and they were created. 6 And he established them forever and ever; he gave a decree, and it will not pass away. 7 Praise Yahweh from the earth, sea creatures and all depths, 8 fire and hail, snow and vapor, wind of tempest, doing his word, 9 mountains and all hills, tree of fruit and all cedars, 10 the living creature and every beast, the creeper and the bird of wing, 11 kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all judges of the earth, 12 young men and also young women, old men with youths. 13 Let them praise the name of Yahweh, for his name alone is exalted; his splendor is over the earth and the heavens. 14 And he has raised up a horn for his people, praise for all of his faithful, for the sons of Israel, a people near to him. Hallelu Yah!

Hallelu Yah! Sing to Yahweh a new song, his praise in the assembly of the faithful. 2 Let Israel rejoice in his Maker; let the sons of Zion be glad in their king. 3 Let them praise his name in the dance; let them make music to him with the tambourine and the harp. 4 For Yahweh is taking pleasure in his people; he beautifies the humble with salvation. 5 Let the faithful exult in glory; let them sing joyfully on their beds. 6 Let the exaltations of God be in their throat, and a sword of mouths in their hand, 7 to execute vengeance on the nations, punishments on the peoples, 8 to bind their kings with chains and their nobles with shackles of iron, 9 to execute on them the judgment written. It is honor to all of his faithful. Hallelu Yah!

Hallelu Yah! Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in the expanse of his strength. 2 Praise him for his mighty deeds; praise him according to the abundance of his greatness. 3 Praise him with the blast of a shofar; praise him with the lyre and the harp. 4 Praise him with the tambourine and the dance; praise him with stringed instruments and the flute. 5 Praise him with cymbals of hearing; praise him with cymbals of a shout. 6 Let all of the breath praise Yah! Hallelu Yah!
